cybersecurity_remaining_courses.title: Cybersecurity Law and Policy
cybersecurity_remaining_courses.description: Comprehensive study of legal frameworks, regulations, and policies governing cybersecurity. Students learn about cybercrime laws, privacy regulations, and compliance requirements while understanding the intersection of law, policy, and cybersecurity practice.
learning_objectives: Understand cybersecurity legal frameworks and regulations
Analyze privacy laws and data protection requirements
Evaluate cybercrime laws and enforcement mechanisms
Develop organizational cybersecurity policies
Assess international cybersecurity law and cooperation
covered_materials: Cybercrime laws and computer fraud statutes
Data protection and privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA)
Sector-specific compliance requirements (HIPAA, SOX, PCI DSS)
Legal issues in cyber warfare and national security

cybersecurity_remaining_courses.title: Security Governance and Compliance
cybersecurity_remaining_courses.description: Development and implementation of cybersecurity governance frameworks and compliance programs. Students learn to align security initiatives with business objectives while meeting regulatory requirements and industry standards.
learning_objectives: Develop cybersecurity governance frameworks
Implement compliance management programs
Conduct security audits and assessments
Manage cybersecurity metrics and reporting
Align security strategy with business objectives
covered_materials: Security metrics and key performance indicators

cybersecurity_remaining_courses.title: Threat Intelligence
cybersecurity_remaining_courses.description: Collection, analysis, and application of cyber threat intelligence to improve organizational security posture. Students learn intelligence gathering techniques, threat analysis methodologies, and how to operationalize threat intelligence for defensive purposes.
learning_objectives: Collect and analyze cyber threat intelligence
Understand threat actor motivations and capabilities
Implement threat intelligence sharing and collaboration
Apply threat intelligence to security operations
Develop strategic and tactical intelligence products
covered_materials: Indicators of compromise (IOCs) and threat hunting

cybersecurity_remaining_courses.title: Vulnerability Assessment
cybersecurity_remaining_courses.description: Systematic identification and evaluation of security vulnerabilities in systems, networks, and applications. Students learn vulnerability scanning techniques, assessment methodologies, and remediation prioritization strategies.
learning_objectives: Conduct comprehensive vulnerability assessments
Use automated scanning tools and manual testing techniques
Prioritize vulnerabilities based on risk and impact
Develop vulnerability management programs
Validate and verify vulnerability remediation efforts
covered_materials: Vulnerability scoring and risk rating systems

cybersecurity_remaining_courses.title: Security Awareness and Training
cybersecurity_remaining_courses.description: Development and implementation of cybersecurity awareness and training programs for organizations. Students learn to design effective security education initiatives, measure training effectiveness, and create a security-conscious organizational culture.
learning_objectives: Design comprehensive security awareness programs
Develop engaging training content and delivery methods
Measure training effectiveness and behavior change
Create security culture and positive behavioral outcomes
Implement phishing simulation and testing programs
covered_materials: Adult learning principles and training design
Phishing simulation and social engineering testing
Metrics and measurement of training effectiveness

cybersecurity_remaining_courses.title: Programming for Security
cybersecurity_remaining_courses.description: Programming concepts and techniques specifically applied to cybersecurity contexts including security tool development, automation, and scripting. Students learn to create security utilities and automate security tasks using various programming languages.
learning_objectives: Develop security tools and utilities using programming languages
Automate security tasks and processes through scripting
Understand secure coding practices and principles
Implement cryptographic functions and security protocols
Create custom security monitoring and analysis tools
covered_materials: Log analysis and security data processing
Malware analysis and reverse engineering scripts

cybersecurity_remaining_courses.title: Database Security
cybersecurity_remaining_courses.description: Security principles and practices for database systems including access control, encryption, and audit mechanisms. Students learn to secure database environments and protect sensitive data while maintaining performance and availability.
learning_objectives: Implement database access controls and authentication
Apply encryption and data protection techniques
Configure database auditing and monitoring
Understand database vulnerabilities and attack vectors
Design secure database architectures and configurations
covered_materials: NoSQL and big data security considerations

cybersecurity_remaining_courses.title: Cybercrime Investigation
cybersecurity_remaining_courses.description: Investigation techniques and procedures for cybercrime cases including evidence collection, legal processes, and coordination with law enforcement. Students learn the intersection of cybersecurity, criminal justice, and digital forensics.
learning_objectives: Understand cybercrime types and investigation procedures
Apply legal frameworks to cybercrime investigation
Coordinate with law enforcement and legal professionals
Collect and preserve digital evidence properly
Understand international cybercrime cooperation mechanisms
covered_materials: Types of cybercrime and criminal behavior online
Legal authorities and jurisdiction in cybercrime
Digital evidence collection and chain of custody
Expert witness testimony and court proceedings

cybersecurity_remaining_courses.title: Applied Mathematics for Security
cybersecurity_remaining_courses.description: Mathematical concepts and techniques applied to cybersecurity including cryptography, probability, statistics, and algorithmic analysis. Students develop mathematical foundations essential for advanced cybersecurity topics.
learning_objectives: Apply mathematical concepts to cryptographic systems
Understand probability and statistics in security contexts
Analyze algorithms and computational complexity
Use mathematical modeling for security problems
Evaluate mathematical foundations of security protocols
covered_materials: Graph theory applications in network security
